key.name: history-size
key.summary: Number of clipboard entries to keep
key.description: Maximum number of recent clipboard entries kept in the history. Mirrors Diodon's recent-items-size.

key.name: use-clipboard
key.description: Watch the regular clipboard (Ctrl+C / Ctrl+X) and record its content.

key.name: use-primary
key.description: Watch the primary selection (text highlighted with the mouse) and record its content. Mirrors Diodon's use-primary.

key.name: synchronize-clipboards
key.summary: Keep CLIPBOARD and PRIMARY in sync
key.description: When either selection changes, copy its content into the other so both hold the same value. Mirrors Diodon's synchronize-clipboards.

key.name: keep-clipboard-content
key.description: Save the history to disk and restore it on the next login, and re-own the last entry so it survives the application that produced it closing. Mirrors Diodon's keep-clipboard-content.

key.name: add-images
key.description: Also record images copied to the clipboard, not only text. Mirrors Diodon's add-images. Off by default because images are larger.

key.name: filter-pattern
key.summary: Ignore content matching this regular expression
key.description: JavaScript regular expression; clipboard text matching it is not added to the history. The default ignores whitespace-only content. Mirrors Diodon's filter-pattern.

key.name: instant-paste
key.description: After an entry is chosen it is copied to the clipboard and Ctrl+V is typed into the previously focused window through GNOME Shell's in-process virtual input device, so no Remote Desktop dialog appears. When off, the entry is only copied and you paste manually. Mirrors Diodon's instant-paste.

key.name: default-paste-keybinding
key.description: Accelerator typed for instant paste in applications without a specific entry in paste-keybindings. Standard paste is Ctrl+V.

key.name: paste-keybindings
key.description: Each entry is "WM_CLASS|accelerator". When instant paste targets a window whose WM_CLASS matches, the given accelerator is typed instead of the default one. Used for terminals, which paste with Shift+Ctrl+V. Mirrors Diodon's app-paste-keybindings.

key.name: show-indicator
key.description: Show the clipboard icon in the top bar. The history is also reachable via the toggle-menu shortcut.

key.name: toggle-menu
key.summary: Shortcut to open the history menu
key.description: Keyboard shortcut that opens the clipboard history menu and focuses the search entry.

key.name: poll-interval-ms
key.description: Wayland does not notify extensions of clipboard changes, so the clipboard is polled on this interval. Lower values capture changes sooner at a small CPU cost. This setting has no equivalent in Diodon, which runs on X11.
